items.title: China’s Top Cybersecurity Firms Hit by Mounting Military Procurement Bans
items.summary:
Chinese cybersecurity firms are facing action from the country’s military, but it’s not due to product or technical failures.
The post China’s Top Cybersecurity Firms Hit by Mounting Military Procurement Bans appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Old UEFI Shims Expose Systems to Secure Boot Bypass
items.summary:
Signed by Microsoft, the vulnerable UEFI shim bootloaders could be abused on any system, regardless of the OS.
The post Old UEFI Shims Expose Systems to Secure Boot Bypass appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Zoom Patches Critical Windows Flaw That Could Enable Account Takeover
items.summary:
Zoom has released security updates for a critical security flaw impacting Zoom Workplace for Windows that could facilitate account takeover.

The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-53412 (CVSS score: 9.8), affects Zoom Desktop Client for Windows, Zoom VDI Client for Windows, and Zoom Meeting SDK for Windows.

"Improper Input Validation in Zoom Desktop Client for Windows, Zoom VDI Client for

items.title: Police Disrupt a €140M Cyber Fraud Ring in Spain
items.summary: Iberian hackers carried out a variety of cyberattacks and laundered the winnings through complex financial networks.

items.title: Nightmare Eclipse Drops ‘LegacyHive’ Windows Zero-Day
items.summary:
The researcher stripped the proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit to prevent immediate exploitation of the vulnerability.
The post Nightmare Eclipse Drops ‘LegacyHive’ Windows Zero-Day  appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Trend Micro, Tanium, ESET and Tenable Patch Severe Product Vulnerabilities
items.summary:
The cybersecurity companies patched critical and high-severity vulnerabilities in some of their products.
The post Trend Micro, Tanium, ESET and Tenable Patch Severe Product Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Applied Computing wants to give oil and gas operators an AI model for the entire plant
items.summary: Applied Computing has raised a $20M Series A to build a foundation AI model for the oil, gas and petrochemical industry.

items.title: Mermaid to Unicode box art (grok-mermaid)
items.summary:
Tool: Mermaid to Unicode box art (grok-mermaid)
        While exploring the codebase for the newly open-sourced Grok CLI coding agent I came across xai-grok-markdown/src/mermaid.rs, a "self-contained terminal renderer for Mermaid diagrams" written in Rust.
I figured it would be fun to try that out in a browser via WebAssembly. Here's the prompt I ran in Claude Code for web (Fable 5), and this is what the resulting tool looks like:

items.title: Microsoft is reportedly training salespeople to talk down OpenAI and Anthropic
items.summary: Microsoft is looking to sell its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective than its competitors' models.

items.title: xai-org/grok-build, now open source
items.summary:
xai-org/grok-build, now open source
xAI's grok CLI tool faced severe community backlash yesterday when it became apparent that running the command in a directory could upload that entire directory to xAI's Google Cloud buckets. One user reported running it in their home directory and seeing it upload "my SSH keys, my password manager database, my documents, photos, videos, everything".
I've not seen an official explanation for why it was doing this, but xAI did respond to the feedback (Musk: "As a precautionary measure, all user data that was uploaded to SpaceXAI before now will be completely

items.title: The npm Threat Landscape: Attack Surface and Mitigations (Updated July 15)
items.summary:
Unit 42 analyzes npm supply chain evolution post-Shai Hulud. Discover wormable malware, CI/CD persistence, multi-stage attacks and more. 
The post The npm Threat Landscape: Attack Surface and Mitigations (Updated July 15) appeared first on Unit 42.

items.title: Dutch police bust investment fraud ring stealing over €100 million
items.summary: The Dutch Police announced the arrest of multiple individuals suspected of being part of an international investment fraud scheme estimated to have tens of thousands of victims. [...]

items.title: xAI sues a man for using Grok to generate CSAM ‘deepfakes’
items.summary: The Elon Musk-owned xAI is suing a South Carolina man who allegedly used the company's Grok AI chatbot to generate child sexual abuse material (CSAM). In a lawsuit reported earlier by Reuters, xAI claims Terry Wayne Harwood "knowingly and intentionally used Grok to circumvent safeguards, alter nonconsensual images, and generate and distribute CSAM," breaching the […]

items.title: Forgotten Bootloaders Expose Secure Boot Blind Spot
items.summary: Nearly a dozen vulnerable and now revoked UEFI shim bootloaders remained trusted for years, giving attackers a path to bypass Secure Boot.

items.title: Identity Attacks Overtake Exploits as Top Ransomware Cause
items.summary: Email attacks overtook exploits as the top ransomware root cause last year. Multifactor authentication (MFA) was deployed in 97% of credential-based attacks but failed to prevent compromise.

items.title: Zoom warns of critical account takeover vulnerability
items.summary: Zoom is warning of a critical vulnerability in its desktop client and software development kit for Windows that could be exploited by an unauthenticated party to hijack accounts. [...]

items.title: Trump administration unveils AI-supported clearinghouse for cyber vulnerabilities
items.summary: The Gold Eagle program will allowe industry, critical infrastructure operators and the government to use artificial intelligence to rapidly detect, prioritize and patch cybersecurity vulnerabilities, officials said.

items.title: AI slop movies are the new direct-to-video cash grabs
items.summary: This weekend, cinephiles across the world will march to their local theaters to feast their eyes on Christopher Nolan's new adaptation of The Odyssey. It's on track to rake in anywhere between $80-$100 million in just a few days. People are clearly excited to see how Nolan uses cutting-edge filmmaking tech to make the Homeric […]

items.title: Amid hardware legal battle, OpenAI releases a $230 keyboard for Codex
items.summary: OpenAI, which is in the middle of a legal battle with Apple over hardware trade theft allegations, just released a light-up keyboard designed to be paired with its agentic coding app.
